name: Young systemic environment restored aged progenitor responses in mice
summary: Heterochronic parabiosis moved aged mouse muscle and liver progenitor responses toward youthful repair behavior.
statement: Exposure of aged mice to a young systemic environment restored muscle satellite-cell and liver progenitor responses after injury, supporting the idea that circulating milieu can modulate age-impaired tissue repair.
population_or_model: Aged mice surgically paired with young mice
time_horizon: Parabiosis exposure followed by tissue injury and repair assays
quantitative_note: The baseline treats this as foundational animal evidence for systemic-milieu modulation, not as a deployable human intervention.
caveats: Parabiosis is a surgical animal model rather than a clinical procedure.
The study does not isolate a single actionable circulating factor.
Animal tissue repair does not establish human healthy-aging benefit.
